behavior_summary: This script collection implements in-game book/page handlers: each function prepares metadata for a specific book entry, constructs a literal text payload (poem, lore, or spell description), and invokes the engine Book::read routine to display that text to the player.
After Book::read returns the handler frees the temporary string, propagates the return value, and then suspends/ends the script execution for that read event.
Handlers are event-driven: they run in response to a player (or actor) activating/reading the associated book object and do not perform world-state logic beyond preparing and displaying text.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BOOK1::BOOK1::0080 (main) and most helpers follow the sequence: set book metadata -> push pid (player id) -> push literal text -> str-to-ptr -> call Book::read(text, object_handle) -> free temporary string -> push/return Book::read retval -> suspend.
U8P_BOOK1::BOOK1::029A appears to be an exception: it sets metadata using the object handle and returns immediately (no Book::read call observed).
No global flags are written/read by the functions in this file (behavior is self-contained display logic).
open_questions: Is BP+06h always a book object handle or, in some cases, a different calling-context PID? The code consistently forwards it to Book::read but exact type is inferred.
What exact engine info is set by the 'set metadata' operations (opcode constants are observed but their semantic names are not fully canonicalized here)?

Are any of these book handlers reachable by automated triggers (timers/area events) or strictly by explicit player activation? The disassembly strongly suggests activation handlers but exact entry mapping is inferred.

main_function.name: U8P_BOOK1::0080
main_function.purpose: Handle the player 'read' activation for a particular book entry: set book metadata, push player id and a long multi-line string (poem/lore), call Book::read to display the text, free the temporary string, return the Book::read result, and suspend.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player (or relevant actor) activates/reads the corresponding book object (inferred from push-of-pid and call to Book::read).

function_map.role: Metadata-only handler: sets info for the book/object and returns without invoking Book::read (appears to be a minimal entry).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script collection implements in-game book/page handlers: each function prepares metadata for a specific book entry, constructs a literal text payload (poem, lore, or spell description), and invokes the engine Book::read routine to display that text to the player.
